metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students develop advanced woodworking skills including joinery, furniture construction, and wood finishing. This course explores wood properties, design principles, and creation of functional and aesthetically pleasing wooden objects.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in advanced joinery, furniture construction, finishing, and wood species knowledge.

big_ideas.statement: Wood is a versatile, renewable material whose properties vary by species and grain structure, affecting its suitability for different purposes.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding wood characteristics—strength, stability, workability—enables appropriate material selection and design for woodworking projects.

big_ideas.statement: Joinery techniques create strong, durable connections that are essential to quality furniture construction and woodwork.
big_ideas.elaboration: Mastery of traditional and modern joinery methods enables creation of structurally sound and aesthetically refined wooden objects.

big_ideas.statement: Furniture design requires balancing aesthetic, functional, and structural considerations while respecting material limitations.
big_ideas.elaboration: Thoughtful design that considers wood movement, construction methods, and use creates furniture that is beautiful and enduring.

big_ideas.statement: Finishing and surface treatments protect wood while enhancing its natural beauty and character.
big_ideas.elaboration: Appropriate finishing techniques preserve wood durability while showcasing grain, colour, and texture.

competencies.raw: Design wooden furniture and objects considering material and construction
i_can_statements: I can sketch and develop design concepts
I can design for wood movement and expansion
I can design joinery that is both functional and aesthetic
I can design with material constraints and sustainability

competencies.raw: Plan construction sequences and joinery approaches
i_can_statements: I can select appropriate joinery for designs
I can plan construction sequences
I can design for grain direction and stability
I can create construction drawings and specifications

competencies.raw: Design finishing and surface treatment approaches
i_can_statements: I can select finishing methods for different applications
I can design surface treatments
I can plan refinishing procedures
I can consider environmental and health factors

competencies.raw: Evaluate woodwork quality and recommend improvements
i_can_statements: I can assess woodworking craftsmanship
I can identify structural and finishing issues
I can analyze joinery effectiveness
I can recommend design and construction improvements

competencies.raw: Select and prepare wood materials appropriately
i_can_statements: I can identify wood species and characteristics
I can assess wood quality and suitability
I can prepare wood through milling and smoothing
I can handle and store wood properly

competencies.raw: Execute traditional and modern joinery techniques
i_can_statements: I can cut mortise and tenon joints
I can create dovetail joints
I can perform edge and surface joinery
I can use biscuits and dowels appropriately

competencies.raw: Assemble furniture using proper gluing and clamping
i_can_statements: I can select appropriate adhesives
I can assemble components with proper gluing
I can clamp assemblies for strength and alignment
I can check assemblies for square and level
I can prepare surfaces for finishing
I can apply stains and dyes
I can apply protective finishes
I can perform refinishing and restoration
I can use saws for accurate cutting
I can use hand planes for smoothing
I can use chisels for detail work
I can sharpen and maintain hand tools

competencies.raw: Operate power tools safely and effectively
i_can_statements: I can operate table saws and band saws
I can use jointers and planers
I can operate routers and sanders
I can maintain and troubleshoot power tools

competencies.raw: Apply wood science knowledge to woodworking
i_can_statements: I can understand wood movement and stability
I can select finishes appropriate for exposure
I can understand wood grain and figure
I can apply sustainable forestry principles
I can assess CNC and machine technologies
I can understand new finishing products
I can evaluate sustainable wood alternatives
I can identify woodworking innovations

areas.name: Wood Selection and Properties
topics: Hardwoods and softwoods: species and characteristics

areas.name: Traditional and Advanced Joinery
topics: Mortise and tenon joints: variations and applications
Dovetail joints: through, half-blind, and sliding

areas.name: Furniture Construction and Assembly
topics: Practical construction of chairs, tables, and cabinets
